User Story: As a visitor, I want the root URL to send me to the correct entry point so I do not land on an irrelevant screen.
Expected Behavior: An unauthenticated request resolves to sign-in; an active Educator resolves to assigned children; an active Admin resolves to pilot access.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - root entry resolves by authentication and role

User Story: As a provisioned Educator, I want to establish a session so I can reach my assigned work.
Expected Behavior: Selecting an active Educator profile creates a signed HTTP-only session and navigates to assigned children or a validated return path.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/platform-smoke.spec.ts - educator navigation and review decision editing work

User Story: As a provisioned Admin, I want to establish an Admin session so I can manage access and failed jobs.
Expected Behavior: Selecting the active Admin profile creates a session and navigates to pilot access with Admin-only navigation.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/platform-smoke.spec.ts - administrator can inspect access and jobs without overflow

User Story: As staff, I want a failed sign-in to explain that access could not be confirmed without exposing account details.
Expected Behavior: A rejected or interrupted sign-in retains no secret, restores available controls, and shows the same non-enumerating error.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-10 auth-access-unavailable

User Story: As signed-in staff, I want the application to identify my current role without exposing protected identity-provider data.
Expected Behavior: GET session returns only the public user projection; missing, forged, expired, or deactivated sessions return 401.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - health and public errors contain no protected runtime configuration; lib/help-review/server-auth.test.ts

User Story: As signed-in staff, I want to sign out so the current browser session can no longer access protected records.
Expected Behavior: Sign out clears the session cookie, replaces the current route with sign-in, and subsequent protected requests fail.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - support is actionable and sign-out clears the session

User Story: As staff whose session ended, I want to sign in again and safely resume my authorized route.
Expected Behavior: The expiry page explains that saved work remains, preserves only a safe local return path, and routes through sign-in.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts - sign-in reflows; tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-11 session-expired

User Story: As staff, I want post-login navigation to preserve only application-local routes so an attacker cannot redirect me externally.
Expected Behavior: Approved relative paths and route-specific query parameters survive; external, malformed, and protected query data fall back to the role home.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - signed sessions origins roles and return paths fail closed; lib/help-review/return-path.test.ts

User Story: As an administrator, I want deactivation to take effect on the next request so removed staff cannot continue using an old session.
Expected Behavior: Every session check also requires an active provision; deactivation causes the next request to return 401.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - assignment revocation and access deactivation take effect on the next request

User Story: As a user without access, I should not learn whether a protected child, assessment, video, or final record exists.
Expected Behavior: Missing and unauthorized protected resources use the same 404/public unavailable presentation and retain the internal reason only in redacted support records.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-15 resource-unavailable; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - assignment revocation

User Story: As staff, I want navigation that contains only commands relevant to my role.
Expected Behavior: Educators see Children and Assessments; Admins see Access and Jobs; hidden links do not replace server authorization.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/platform-smoke.spec.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - Admin access and processing support commands are scoped

User Story: As staff, I want the current section visibly identified so I can orient myself.
Expected Behavior: The active primary navigation link is visually distinct and exposes aria-current page.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - current navigation is announced and sandbox boundary persists
Observed Errors: Active navigation initially omitted aria-current.
Fix Evidence: Added aria-current=page to the active Educator and Admin primary links.

User Story: As staff, I want a support entry point when I cannot resolve access or processing problems.
Expected Behavior: The header and relevant empty/error states expose the configured organization-owned pilot-support mail action without record data; when no valid recipient is configured, the UI shows non-linked guidance instead of a broken mail action.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts; lib/help-review/support-contact.test.ts; lib/help-review/runtime-config.test.ts
Observed Errors: Initial execution found a reserved recipient; the later manual keyboard pass found that an unset recipient still produced a recipient-free mailto link.
Fix Evidence: Centralized validation now returns no mail action for an unset, malformed, or reserved recipient; all surfaces suppress the link while keeping plain guidance, production requires an organization-owned address, and browser tests use a valid synthetic recipient.

User Story: As a development user, I want a persistent indication that the environment contains sanitized pilot data.
Expected Behavior: The sanitized environment banner remains visible across application routes and does not overlap navigation or content.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts; tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts

User Story: As an Educator, I want to see only children currently assigned to me.
Expected Behavior: The list API and UI return active assigned children only, ordered deterministically, with minimum approved child context.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - child history search filters and stale next actions stay assignment-scoped

User Story: As an Educator, I want clear feedback while assignments are loading.
Expected Behavior: The page announces that active assignments are being checked and does not show stale rows or enabled row actions.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - child loading and recovery never expose stale assignments

User Story: As an Educator with several assignments, I want to filter the list by child identifier.
Expected Behavior: Search is case-insensitive, updates visible rows without changing access scope, and preserves the original list when cleared.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - assigned-child search filters locally and explains no matches

User Story: As an Educator, I want a useful state when no assigned child matches my search.
Expected Behavior: The page distinguishes no search results from no assignments and suggests changing the identifier.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - assigned-child search filters locally and explains no matches

User Story: As an Educator without assignments, I want to understand why the list is empty and what to do next.
Expected Behavior: The empty state shows no child data, explains administrator assignment, and offers refresh.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-12 assigned-children-empty

User Story: As an Educator, I want to retry a temporary assignment-list failure or sign out.
Expected Behavior: The failure state discloses no records, offers retry and sign-out, and replaces itself with authoritative data after recovery.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - child loading and recovery never expose stale assignments; screen-13

User Story: As an Educator, I want each child row to take me to the authoritative next step for the latest assessment.
Expected Behavior: Rows show current status/date and route to upload, processing, review, summary, or final according to server-derived state.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - child history search filters and stale next actions stay assignment-scoped

User Story: As an Educator, I want to inspect an assigned child's approved context and assessment history.
Expected Behavior: The page shows identifier, age, optional context, processing-permission warning, and only that child's authorized assessments.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-14 child-detail-history-v2; workflow assignment-scoped test

User Story: As an Educator, I want loading and retry behavior when a child detail request is interrupted.
Expected Behavior: The page announces loading, shows a safe failure without stale data, and retries the same authorized child.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - failed child detail load retries without stale content

User Story: As an Educator, I want to start an observation from an assigned child when processing is permitted.
Expected Behavior: The action opens intake for that child; when permission is off it opens the blocked intake state instead of accepting a video.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts - child navigation and intake; tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-20 permission-blocked

User Story: As an Educator, I want a single list of my authorized assessments so I can continue or reopen work.
Expected Behavior: The index shows child, observation date, status, progress, last update, and the authoritative action for assignment-scoped assessments.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-16 assessments-list

User Story: As an Educator, I want to switch between active, finalized, and all assessments.
Expected Behavior: The selected filter is URL-backed, aria-pressed, and the server returns only the requested authorized statuses.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - assessment filters and search round-trip through URL; workflow scope tests

User Story: As an Educator, I want to search assessments by child identifier.
Expected Behavior: Submitting search writes a bounded query to the URL, scopes the server query to assignments, and clearing restores the filter result.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - assessment filters and search round-trip through URL; workflow bounded query test

Feature: Assessment loading error and empty states
User Story: As an Educator, I want distinct feedback for loading, no matches, and request failure.
Expected Behavior: Loading disables rows; no matches offers assigned children; failure offers retry without showing stale data.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - assessment failures and empty results remain distinct and recoverable

User Story: As an Educator, I want one draft per create request so retries cannot create duplicate assessments.
Expected Behavior: Creation requires assignment and processing permission, snapshots child context and contract versions, and replays the same request ID while rejecting changed payload reuse.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - assessment creation is authorized versioned and idempotent

User Story: As an Educator, I want the observation date attached to the assessment before processing.
Expected Behavior: A valid date is required to create the draft and becomes immutable in intake after the assessment exists.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - invalid observation date is rejected and persisted draft locks the date

User Story: As an Educator following an old link, I want the system to redirect me to the assessment's current state.
Expected Behavior: An intake URL for a processing, review, summary, or finalized assessment resolves to its authoritative route rather than rendering stale controls.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - child history search filters and stale next actions stay assignment-scoped

User Story: As an Educator, I want finalized assessments to reopen as immutable records.
Expected Behavior: Index and child history route finalized assessments to final view; review mutations and ordinary editing remain unavailable.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - review decisions survive reload reject stale edits and leave final records immutable

User Story: As an Educator, I want to confirm the child and observation context before uploading a video.
Expected Behavior: Intake shows the assigned child, current age/context, observation date, one-video policy, and approved limits before submission.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-17 upload-ready; tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts - child navigation and intake

User Story: As an Educator, I must not upload when processing permission is unavailable.
Expected Behavior: The intake shows a non-editable blocked state with administrator contact and return navigation; upload and processing APIs reject the command.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-20 permission-blocked; workflow assessment creation permission test

User Story: As an Educator, I want to choose one MP4, WebM, or MOV observation video.
Expected Behavior: The file picker limits selectable media, displays filename and size, and offers discard or choose-another before upload.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - file choice is inspectable discardable and rejects invalid local files

User Story: As an Educator, I want immediate feedback when the chosen file is unsupported, too large, empty, or too long.
Expected Behavior: Client inspection rejects non-video types, empty or over-100-MB files, and known durations over five minutes before upload while retaining intake context.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - invalid local files; screen-18; lib/help-review/video-policy.test.ts

User Story: As the system, I must reject renamed or corrupted files even when browser metadata claims they are videos.
Expected Behavior: Server validation enforces MIME allowlist, actual container signature, declared and actual size, duration, checksum, and strict request schemas.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - upload validation replacement removal and browser-independent processing work; upload commands enforce all sanitized limits

User Story: As an Educator, I want progress feedback while a selected video is being uploaded.
Expected Behavior: Upload disables conflicting actions, announces progress in a labeled progress bar, and settles to authoritative uploaded metadata.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-03 upload-observation

User Story: As an Educator, I want a network interruption to leave the assessment recoverable.
Expected Behavior: The page reports that the network interrupted upload, does not claim success, and permits retry or replacement without creating a processing run.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-19 upload-network-failure

User Story: As the system, I want browsers to upload video directly to private GCS without exposing durable storage credentials.
Expected Behavior: The server issues a short-lived origin-bound resumable session; the browser uploads bytes; completion verifies bucket, opaque object, generation, CRC32C, metadata, size, signature, and signed grant.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/upload-grants.test.ts; lib/help-review/processing-dispatcher.test.ts; live deployment evidence

User Story: As an Educator, I want to change a not-yet-uploaded file without changing the assessment.
Expected Behavior: Choose another replaces only the local selection and leaves the saved video and assessment state unchanged until upload succeeds.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - replacing a selection preserves the verified video until replacement uploads

User Story: As an Educator, I want to replace the uploaded video before processing begins.
Expected Behavior: A successful replacement stores one new asset, deletes or supersedes the old object, resets the public projection, and keeps no duplicate active video.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - local replacement preserves saved asset; workflow replacement and replay test

User Story: As an Educator, I want to remove an uploaded video before processing.
Expected Behavior: Remove is disabled during mutations, deletes the active private asset, returns intake to no-video state, and is idempotent when repeated.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - removing video disables processing; workflow removal and replay test

User Story: As an Educator, I want to leave intake after uploading and resume later.
Expected Behavior: Save and exit returns to the assigned child without starting processing; the uploaded video remains attached to the draft.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - save and exit preserves the verified draft for later return

User Story: As an Educator, I want Start processing enabled only when one verified uploaded video is ready.
Expected Behavior: The action remains disabled during upload, while a replacement is selected, or without an uploaded video; a successful command navigates to processing.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - removing video disables processing and verified video starts it; workflow API test

User Story: As the system, I must create only one active processing run when Start is clicked or delivered repeatedly.
Expected Behavior: Concurrent/replayed commands return the same run, dispatch once through the configured adapter, and never duplicate suggestion sets.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - upload commands enforce all sanitized limits and remain replay-safe

User Story: As an Educator, I want a stable progress view while analysis is queued or running.
Expected Behavior: The page presents uploaded, security, analysis, and review-ready steps with one current state and safe attempt metadata.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-04 processing and screen-36 mobile-processing

User Story: As an Educator, I want processing status to update while I remain on the page.

Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - processing refreshes automatically only while visible

User Story: As an Educator, I want to refresh status when I suspect the displayed state is stale.
Expected Behavior: Refresh shows pending feedback, requests authoritative state, updates the timeline, and reports a recoverable load error without discarding the last known state.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - processing refreshes automatically and on demand

User Story: As an Educator, I want analysis to continue after I close or leave the page.
Expected Behavior: The persisted run is delivered to the standalone processor and reaches ready or safe failure without browser polling.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - upload validation replacement removal and browser-independent processing work

User Story: As an Educator, I want clear confirmation when validated suggestions are ready.

Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-22 processing-ready

User Story: As an Educator, I want a useful failure state without provider internals.

User Story: As an Educator, I want to retry a temporary failure using the existing video.

User Story: As an Educator, I must not retry terminal, permission-lost, missing-video, reviewed, or superseded work.

User Story: As support staff, I want old queued/running attempts converted to a safe stuck failure for follow-up.

User Story: As the system, I want duplicate Eventarc deliveries to be harmless.

User Story: As the system, I want review to open only after a complete model result passes validation.

User Story: As an Educator, I want a minimal review workspace containing the video, validated suggestions, saved decisions, and derived progress.
Expected Behavior: The server returns only the assigned assessment's public child/video/suggestion/decision projection with a short-lived playback URL and no storage/provider internals.

User Story: As an Educator, I want stable skeletons while suggestions, decisions, and video access load.

User Story: As an Educator, I want to retry a temporary review request failure.

User Story: As an Educator, I want an early or stale review URL to resolve to the correct processing/final state.

User Story: As an Educator, I must not see partial or invalid model suggestions.

User Story: As an Educator, I want to select a skill and retain that context across refresh.

User Story: As an Educator, I want to abandon unsaved changes and restore the last saved decision.

Feature: Add a skill the AI missed
User Story: As an Educator, I want to add and score a catalogue skill the AI never surfaced so the final record reflects everything I observed.
Expected Behavior: An add-skill panel offers age-appropriate unsuggested catalogue skills; adding creates the suggestion and its decision atomically with a server-derived MANUALLY_ADDED origin and server-resolved labels; duplicates, unknown skills, and finalized assessments are rejected; the item shows an Added-by-you badge, survives reload, and appears in summary and final records as Added by educator.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/domain.test.ts; lib/help-review/application-services.test.ts - adds an educator skill with its decision atomically; tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - REVIEW-025

User Story: As an Educator, I want a deliberate confirmation before locking the assessment.
Expected Behavior: The dialog identifies included and dismissed counts, explains immutability, traps focus, supports cancel/Escape, and requires explicit confirmation.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - finalization is deliberate; tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts - focus restoration

User Story: As the system, I must finalize only a complete current revision and safely replay the same request.
Expected Behavior: Incomplete or stale commands return conflict; one transaction rederives summary, stamps actor/time once, and repeated request ID returns the same final record.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - summary finalization is complete-only stale-safe and idempotent

User Story: As an Educator, I want to reopen the human-approved assessment without editable controls.
Expected Behavior: Final view shows finalized metadata and the same totals/included/dismissed record, offers Return to child, and contains no save/finalize/edit commands.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-07 final-assessment screen-41 mobile-final; accessibility summary/final test

User Story: As an Administrator, I want access management restricted to authorized administrators.
Expected Behavior: The page and API require an active ADMIN session; educators receive a safe forbidden response and no staff or assignment data.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - admin endpoints enforce role boundaries

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to see provisioned staff and their access state.
Expected Behavior: The directory and selected detail show display name, normalized email, role, active state, and active assignment count without exposing assessment or video data.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - access directory and filters; workflow public projection test

Feature: Access loading failure and empty states
User Story: As an Administrator, I want clear recovery states while the directory loads or cannot load.
Expected Behavior: Loading preserves the page shell; failures offer Try again; an empty directory offers staff provisioning instead of a blank table.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screens 33 and 34

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to find staff by identity and access state.
Expected Behavior: Search matches name or email; the active-state filter composes with search; no-match state keeps both controls editable.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - access filters are URL-backed and failed confirmation remains recoverable

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to provision an educator or administrator before first sign-in.
Expected Behavior: Opening the form focuses its first field; a validated name, normalized email, and role create or update one provision idempotently; errors remain without claiming success; cancel returns focus to the trigger.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - admin access mutations are authorized validated and idempotent; tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - provision form keyboard focus and restoration
Observed Errors: Manual keyboard execution found that cancelling the inline provisioning form moved focus to the document body.
Fix Evidence: The opening trigger is retained, exposes aria-expanded/aria-controls, and receives focus after cancel or successful closure; a keyboard regression test covers open, initial focus, cancel, and restoration.

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to inspect one staff member before changing access.
Expected Behavior: Selecting a row opens a stable detail panel with identity, role, active state, assignments, and only actions allowed for the current state.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screen-30 admin-staff-detail

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to restore a provisioned staff account.
Expected Behavior: Activate updates the authoritative provision, refreshes the directory and detail panel, and permits a future session without duplicating the identity.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - admin access mutations are authorized validated and idempotent

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to revoke staff access deliberately.
Expected Behavior: A confirmation names the staff member; confirmation deactivates the provision, invalidates active sessions, preserves audit/history records, and does not expose or delete child data.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - deactivation revokes sessions without deleting records

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to assign an educator to a child.
Expected Behavior: Only active educators and unassigned eligible children are selectable; the mutation is idempotent and the refreshed count and assignment list match authoritative storage.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - assignment writes enforce active educator and unique relation

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to remove an educator-child assignment without deleting records.
Expected Behavior: A confirmation identifies the child; removal immediately removes roster visibility for new requests while preserving assessments and audit history.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - assignment removal revokes roster visibility and preserves history

User Story: As an Administrator, I want failed access changes to be retryable and truthful.
Expected Behavior: Network, validation, conflict, and server failures keep the affected dialog or panel open, show a safe actionable message, and never optimistically claim that access changed.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - access filters are URL-backed and failed confirmation remains recoverable
Observed Errors: Failed confirmation initially closed before the administrator could retry.
Fix Evidence: Confirmation mutations now await an authoritative success, expose the safe error inside the modal, and close only after a successful refresh.

User Story: As an Administrator, I want provisioning to invite the staff member so they can set their own password without anyone handling credentials.
Expected Behavior: In email/password mode, provisioning and Resend invite issue a single-use hashed setup token, deliver it through the configured email adapter, show delivery status, and never create, display, or transmit a usable password; invitations to inactive staff are rejected.
Code Evidence: lib/help-review/admin-access-service.ts; lib/help-review/email-password-auth.ts; lib/help-review/email-sender.ts; app/api/admin/access/route.ts; app/admin/access/page.tsx; app/set-password/page.tsx
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/email-password-auth.test.ts - issues invites through provisioning and resend only in email/password mode

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to correct a staff member's display name and role.
Expected Behavior: Editing updates the display name and role with actor/time recording and next-request effect; Admins cannot change their own role, and Educators with active child assignments cannot change role until unassigned.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/email-password-auth.test.ts - edits display name and role while protecting the actor and assigned educators

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to remove a staff member entirely while keeping their historical work attributable.
Expected Behavior: Removal immediately revokes access, sessions, assignments, credentials, and outstanding tokens, keeps decisions and finalized records attributed to the retained display name, frees the email for later re-provisioning, and rejects self-removal.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/email-password-auth.test.ts - removes staff, revokes everything, retains attribution, and frees the email

User Story: As an Administrator, I want processing recovery restricted to authorized administrators.
Expected Behavior: The jobs page and APIs require an active ADMIN session; educators receive a safe forbidden response and no processing or video metadata.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - admin endpoints enforce role boundaries

User Story: As an Administrator, I want a safe queue of processing runs that may require attention.
Expected Behavior: The list contains only failed or stale in-progress runs and shows child and assessment labels, status, safe failure category, and last change; attempt history remains in safe detail without raw provider data.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - job filters details and retry confirmation stay safe; workflow projection test

User Story: As an Administrator, I want to narrow recoverable jobs efficiently.
Expected Behavior: Search matches safe assessment or child labels; failed/stuck status filters compose with search; clearing filters restores the authoritative result set.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - job filters details and retry confirmation stay safe

Feature: Job loading failure and empty states
User Story: As an Administrator, I want clear operational states when jobs load, fail, or need no action.
Expected Behavior: Loading preserves the shell; failure offers Try again; no failed jobs communicates healthy state; no filter matches offers reset.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts - screens 35 and 36

User Story: As an Administrator, I want enough safe detail to decide whether retry is appropriate.
Expected Behavior: Selecting a job exposes its expanded state and shows lifecycle timestamps, attempt history, playback availability, safe diagnostics, and retry eligibility without sensitive payloads; closing details returns focus to that job row.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - job filters details, close-focus restoration, and retry confirmation stay safe
Observed Errors: Manual keyboard execution found that closing job details moved focus to the document body.
Fix Evidence: Job rows now expose aria-expanded/aria-controls, the selected trigger is retained, and closing details restores focus; the behavior test verifies the full keyboard close and reopen sequence.

User Story: As an Administrator, I want the platform to prevent harmful or pointless retries.
Expected Behavior: Only failed or stale recoverable runs with an available source video can retry; finalized, actively leased, exhausted, missing-video, and non-retryable failures explain why retry is disabled.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/admin-jobs-service.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts

User Story: As an Administrator, I want retries to require a deliberate confirmation.
Expected Behavior: The confirmation identifies the assessment and current attempt; cancel makes no mutation; confirm submits one idempotent retry request and reports progress.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts
Observed Errors: The retry confirmation initially named only the child.
Fix Evidence: The dialog now names the assessment reference, current attempt, safe category, and private-video availability before confirmation.

User Story: As the system, I must create a retry without overwriting immutable prior attempts.
Expected Behavior: The retry transaction rechecks eligibility, records actor and reason, creates the next attempt, preserves prior history, and dispatches processing only after the durable state exists.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/admin-jobs-service.test.ts; lib/help-review/processing-coordinator.test.ts

User Story: As an Administrator, I want accurate feedback after a retry request.
Expected Behavior: Success refreshes the job state and can be dismissed; conflict or dispatch failure remains visible, preserves history, and allows a valid next action without duplicate attempts.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - retry failure remains actionable and success dismisses; service replay tests

User Story: As approved staff, I want to sign in through the selected production identity path so access does not depend on a sandbox profile.
Expected Behavior: Exactly one first-party email/password path validates credentials server-side against scrypt-hashed application storage, maps active provisioned access only, supports single-use invitation and reset links plus safe session/logout, revokes access promptly, and exposes no public signup.
Code Evidence: lib/help-review/email-password-auth.ts; lib/help-review/email-sender.ts; lib/help-review/server-auth.ts; app/api/session/route.ts; app/api/auth/set-password/route.ts; app/api/auth/request-reset/route.ts; app/set-password/page.tsx; app/sign-in/page.tsx; app/api/admin/access/route.ts; scripts/bootstrap-admin.ts; infra/gcp/main.tf
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/email-password-auth.test.ts; lib/help-review/server-auth.test.ts; lib/help-review/runtime-config.test.ts; tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts - LIVE-001; terraform validate; email-password production build
Observed Errors: Earlier managed-provider defects (Terraform quota prerequisites, unstable GCS service-agent planning, read-only provider IAM, loading copy) were fixed during QA2. On July 15 the managed Google Identity Platform path was replaced by the approved first-party email/password implementation and firebase-admin was removed.
Fix Evidence: The first-party path hashes credentials with scrypt, burns a constant-time verification for unknown accounts, issues single-use hashed invite/reset tokens through the email boundary, auto-invalidates outstanding tokens on password set, keeps sandbox sessions unusable at the email/password issuer boundary, and fails production startup without a real email adapter, sender, and https origin.

User Story: As an Operator, I want approved children, age/context, processing permission, and assignments ingested from one controlled source so real pilot participants can be onboarded and reconciled.
Expected Behavior: A versioned controlled import validates stable child identifiers and approved context, resolves exact already-provisioned Educators, applies idempotent create/update/deactivate behavior, reports missing or duplicate records without echoing values, reconciles explicit assignments and permission changes, records actor/source/time, and preserves assessments plus records omitted from a merge file.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/roster-import.test.ts; tests/fixtures/synthetic-roster.csv; 2026-07-15 CLI preview; clean-schema five-migration check
Observed Errors: Initial audit found that only seeded children could be assigned; no controlled production child/roster ingestion or reconciliation command existed.
Fix Evidence: Added the help-roster-v1 CSV contract, safe parser, dry-run/apply CLI, exact Educator resolution, context-source snapshots, merge reconciliation, child deactivation revocation, checksum replay safety, allowlisted audit event, template, migration, and seven focused tests.

User Story: As a Content owner, I want the platform and scoring boundary to use the authoritative approved HELP catalogue so skill identity, ordering, labels, and final records are consistent.
Expected Behavior: A versioned import consumes authoritative skill IDs, labels, domains/strands, age ranges, hierarchy/order, credit descriptions, and confirmed scoring rules; assessments snapshot the version and unknown or mismatched skills fail closed.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/help-catalog.test.ts; lib/help-review/scoring-contract.test.ts; scripts/validate-help-catalog.ts; 165-test unit suite; production web and processor builds; 20260715-qa1 deployed assessment smoke
Observed Errors: The complete help-catalog-v1 boundary now supports provenance, observable definitions and indicators, non-examples, observation conditions, prohibited inferences, evidence modality, video scoreability, and per-credit criteria. A deterministic, attributed 127-item CDC/ELOF development corpus validates and excludes eight longitudinal-only items from short-video candidate batches. Real-data mode rejects both reference/fixture content; the owner-issued authoritative HELP catalogue itself has not yet been supplied.
Fix Evidence: Added the immutable JSON loader and validator, documented intake contract, Docker packaging, Terraform path/version inputs, startup validation, deployed snapshot check, plus a research-grounded reference corpus and deterministic generator so model work can proceed without fabricating HELP content.

User Story: As the processing system, I want Yi's versioned model package and data contract integrated behind the scoring gateway so deployed results match the scientist-owned workflow.
Expected Behavior: The processor sends only the contracted video reference and child context, identifies request/run/schema versions, validates accepted uncertain empty and failure fixtures, maps safe retry/terminal errors, and contract-tests the exact deployed package or service.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/reference-scorer.test.ts; lib/help-review/scoring-contract.test.ts; processor/server.test.ts; lib/help-review/fake-scoring.test.ts; 165-test unit suite; production web and processor builds; QA1 GCS/Eventarc/Vertex failure-boundary smoke
Observed Errors: The first-party evidence-first-v1 pipeline now separates Gemini video observation from catalogue adjudication, uses structured outputs and exact event/candidate allowlists, rejects non-target or out-of-duration evidence, gates confidence, and prevents absence-only NOT_OBSERVED credits. Vertex defaults to Flash observation and Pro adjudication. Yi's promised package/data contract and accepted fixtures are still not integrated, and the first-party model has not yet been benchmarked against independently educator-labelled videos.
Fix Evidence: Retained the safe Vertex/GCS transport and added a contract-compatible two-stage reference scorer, configurable FPS/models/batching/thresholds, richer candidate guidance, prompt-injection boundaries, 9 focused scorer tests, and all-or-nothing output validation.

User Story: As an Operator, I want approved real-video region, retention, deletion, backup, and incident values applied so private replay evidence is governed throughout its lifecycle.
Expected Behavior: Organization-owned GCS applies the final region and lifecycle values, incomplete uploads and replaced/removed objects are deleted as contracted, authorized range replay remains available for the retention window, backup behavior is explicit, and deletion plus incident paths are exercised.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/video-storage.test.ts; lib/help-review/upload-grants.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts
Observed Errors: Private upload, integrity, replacement/removal, and replay pass with development lifecycle values; final real-video region, retention, backup, deletion, and incident values are not applied and drill-verified.

User Story: As a signed-in user, I need cross-site requests prevented from mutating protected records.
Expected Behavior: Every state-changing browser API validates same-origin intent before parsing or executing the command and returns a safe forbidden response on mismatch.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/server-http.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts

User Story: As the system, I must reject malformed or oversized input before business logic.
Expected Behavior: Strict JSON content type, bounded body size, schema validation, identifier validation, and upload metadata policy return safe 4xx errors without partial writes.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/server-http.test.ts; lib/help-review/video-policy.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts

User Story: As the system, I must limit high-risk or expensive endpoints per authenticated actor.
Expected Behavior: Sign-in, upload grants, process starts, playback grants, admin mutations, and retries apply bounded server-side rate limits with Retry-After and no sensitive response detail.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/server-http.test.ts; lib/help-review/shared-rate-limit.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts; scripts/shared-rate-limit-drill.ts
Observed Errors: The original process-local production budget was replaced. PostgreSQL-backed deployments now atomically share fixed-window actor budgets across instances; local development retains the in-process implementation.
Fix Evidence: Added a hashed shared RateLimitBucket, atomic INSERT ON CONFLICT increments, stale-bucket cleanup, Retry-After preservation, focused tests, and a 12-concurrent-request isolated PostgreSQL drill with complete cleanup.

User Story: As a browser user, I need hardened defaults on pages and API responses.
Expected Behavior: Production responses apply content-type protection, frame denial, referrer policy, permissions policy, CSP, and HSTS where transport is HTTPS without breaking required media playback.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - security headers and health projection

User Story: As an Educator, I must only access children and assessments currently assigned to me.
Expected Behavior: List, detail, upload, processing, review, finalization, final record, and playback APIs reauthorize the current session and assignment on every request; guessed identifiers return no data.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - educator assignment boundaries are enforced across workflow

User Story: As an API consumer, I should receive only fields required by the current screen.
Expected Behavior: Public serializers omit emails where unnecessary, storage object names, provider payloads, credentials, raw model responses, internal errors, and unrestricted child attributes.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - public payloads redact internal and sensitive fields

User Story: As an Educator, I want private playback only for the assessment I am currently reviewing.
Expected Behavior: A short-lived signed grant binds actor, assessment, video version, purpose, and expiry; tampering, expiry, reassignment, or version change invalidates it; byte-range streaming supports seeking.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/playback-grants.test.ts; lib/help-review/video-storage.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts

User Story: As an Administrator, I should not gain source-video or review-content access merely from my admin role.

Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts - admin role does not imply assessment or video access

User Story: As a support operator, I want useful correlation details without child data or secrets.

Fix Evidence: Support event persistence now explicitly projects only the approved diagnostic field allowlist.

User Story: As the system, I must not silently use sandbox identity, memory storage, fake scoring, or local video in production.
Expected Behavior: Production startup validates required database, session, identity, GCS, processor, and scoring settings and exits with a targeted error when an insecure adapter is selected or a secret is missing.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/runtime-config.test.ts; lib/help-review/server-bootstrap.test.ts; app/runtime-boundary.test.ts

User Story: As a pilot user, I want synthetic mode visibly distinguished from any real-data environment.
Expected Behavior: Sandbox mode shows the persistent sanitized-data banner, uses synthetic fixtures only, and does not imply that real child data is accepted when production adapters are absent.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts; app/runtime-boundary.test.ts

User Story: As a pilot stakeholder, I want the approved product boundary enforced rather than placeholder features that imply unsupported capability.
Expected Behavior: No parent portal, longitudinal analytics, report export, direct HELP Connect sync, arbitrary catalog editing, or autonomous final scoring is exposed unless an approved contract and implementation exists.
Automated Evidence: app/runtime-boundary.test.ts; tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts; production build route manifest
Fix Evidence: Expanded the runtime-boundary test to lock parent, analytics, report, catalogue, and HELP Connect sync routes out of the shipped platform.

User Story: As the system, I need durable relational state for users, assignments, assessments, attempts, decisions, audit events, and final records.
Expected Behavior: Production uses the Prisma/PostgreSQL adapter, enforces declared constraints and transactions, and never falls back to process memory.
Automated Evidence: prisma/schema.test.ts; lib/help-review/application-services.test.ts; app/runtime-boundary.test.ts

User Story: As an Operator, I want schema changes applied reproducibly before traffic uses them.
Expected Behavior: Ordered checked-in migrations apply cleanly to an empty database and the configured production database; migration failure prevents release promotion rather than causing runtime drift.
Automated Evidence: prisma/schema.test.ts; 2026-07-15 clean-schema six-migration deploy; pnpm db:migrate; pnpm db:status; pnpm db:recovery-drill; pnpm db:rate-limit-drill
Observed Errors: Initial execution had clean-schema and current-schema evidence but no exercised logical backup and restore.
Fix Evidence: Added guarded isolated-schema drills; all six migrations and one synthetic marker survived pg_dump/pg_restore, 12 concurrent shared increments serialized, and every temporary artifact was removed.

User Story: As the system, I need uploaded videos stored privately with lifecycle controls.
Expected Behavior: Production uploads go directly to the configured private GCS bucket through a constrained signed grant, record one immutable object version, reject untrusted object references, and never make the object public.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/upload-grants.test.ts; lib/help-review/video-storage.test.ts

User Story: As the system, I need a completed upload to trigger processing without relying on a browser request staying open.
Expected Behavior: A GCS object-finalized event reaches the private processor through Eventarc; duplicate or delayed events are safe and the assessment records durable dispatch and attempt state.
Automated Evidence: processor/server.test.ts; lib/help-review/processing-dispatcher.test.ts

User Story: As the system, I need the scorer invoked with the approved video and contract version.
Expected Behavior: The processor resolves the recorded GCS URI, verifies the object generation, sends the versioned structured scoring contract to Vertex AI Gemini, and never treats prose as the canonical result.
Automated Evidence: processor/server.test.ts; lib/help-review/scoring-contract.test.ts

User Story: As the system, I need untrusted model output validated before it becomes a review draft.
Expected Behavior: Only known HELP skill IDs, allowed credit values, bounded confidence, valid timestamp ranges, and the expected contract version are accepted; invalid or empty output produces a safe recoverable state rather than fabricated suggestions.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/scoring-contract.test.ts; lib/help-review/server-workflow.test.ts

User Story: As the system, I need duplicate delivery and transient failure handled without concurrent conflicting runs.
Expected Behavior: A durable lease admits one active worker, heartbeats or expires safely, bounds attempts and exponential retry, preserves immutable attempt history, and makes terminal completion idempotent.
Automated Evidence: lib/help-review/processing-coordinator.test.ts; lib/help-review/server-workflow.test.ts; processor/server.test.ts

User Story: As a Developer, I want the web application and processor to run locally with synthetic video and scoring.
Expected Behavior: Documented commands start both processes, use local private storage and deterministic fake scoring, exercise the same contracts and state transitions, and do not require Google Cloud credentials.

Fix Evidence: Started pnpm dev:stack, verified both health endpoints, uploaded the synthetic fixture, dispatched over HTTP, and observed READY_FOR_REVIEW with eight suggestions.

User Story: As an Operator, I want safe health signals for routing and diagnosis.
Expected Behavior: Web and processor health checks distinguish process liveness from required dependency readiness, return non-success when a required dependency is unavailable, and expose no secrets or child data.
Automated Evidence: processor/server.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts

User Story: As an Operator, I want request and processing events correlated without collecting sensitive content.
Expected Behavior: Logs and support events use correlation, assessment, run, attempt, adapter, latency, and safe error-category fields; they never log video URLs, access tokens, model payloads, notes, names, or uploaded filenames.
Automated Evidence: processor/server.test.ts; lib/help-review/server-events.test.ts; tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts; terraform validate; QA1 deployed log inspection and synthetic metric signal; clean final plan
Observed Errors: QA1 now has both metrics, policies, an enabled development email channel, dashboard, safe deployed outcome inspection, and a payload-free signal with metric value one. The final organization operations address and notification receipt still require owner acceptance.
Fix Evidence: Deployed the observability stack; verified the structured Vertex failure contained only allowlisted fields; generated a synthetic metric signal; fixed fresh-project metric propagation with a one-time 90-second guard; suppressed only server-injected dashboard JSON drift; final Terraform refresh is clean.

User Story: As an Operator, I want organization-owned infrastructure described as code.
Expected Behavior: Terraform and Cloud Build define private GCS, Eventarc, Cloud Run web and processor services, service accounts, least-privilege IAM, secrets, database connectivity, health checks, and immutable image deployment.
Automated Evidence: terraform fmt/validate; Cloud Builds 3e2638c3-4665-4d27-96f4-2d2ba7122cda and 7e8bd1d7-a3e0-40ab-94fc-e91a39e1a442; QA1 provider smoke; QA2 managed identity/rollback/rotation; final no-change plan
Observed Errors: The exact sanitized QA2 candidate is applied to the development project with managed identity, immutable digests, Ready services, provider lifecycle, recovery drills, and a clean plan. Organization-owned staging/production and backend handoff remain unrecorded; Neon is in AWS us-east-1 and still needs explicit acceptance or Cloud SQL replacement for an all-GCP boundary.
Fix Evidence: QA2 fixed quota-project attribution, declared Cloud Resource Manager, stabilized the GCS service-agent IAM address, restored Eventarc publishing, granted the web service account only the provider user-lifecycle role it needs, and ignores only Cloud Run operational revision metadata. Final refresh is clean with IAM and services Ready.

User Story: As a Release manager, I want releases proven reversible before real-data launch.
Expected Behavior: The runbook covers migration order, smoke checks, traffic promotion, application rollback, database restore, failed-job recovery, key rotation, and incident escalation with recorded staging evidence.
Automated Evidence: six-migration isolated logical backup/restore; 12-concurrent-increment PostgreSQL drill; QA2 immutable rollback/roll-forward; session-secret rotation, old-cookie rejection, recovery sign-in; final no-change plan
Observed Errors: The first recovery pass lacked application rollback and secret rotation, and an out-of-band rotation revision produced harmless operational metadata drift.
Fix Evidence: QA2 rolled back to QA1 and forward in 44 seconds each with health and managed-session continuity, rotated the session secret in 14 seconds, rejected the old cookie, restored Google sign-in, retained the failed-run/metric and six-migration logical restore evidence, and ended on a zero-change Terraform plan. Final notification receipt remains tracked by RUNTIME-010 rather than release reversibility.

User Story: As a Keyboard user, I want every workflow operable without a pointer.
Expected Behavior: All interactive controls have visible focus, logical order, native semantics, no keyboard trap outside open dialogs, Escape where appropriate, and focus restoration after dialogs or mobile overlays close.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts; tests/e2e/user-story-behaviors.spec.ts; 2026-07-15 all-workflow manual keyboard execution
Observed Errors: The first automated audit found a mobile review focus defect; the manual full Educator and Admin keyboard pass then found lost focus after cancelling staff provisioning and closing job details.
Fix Evidence: The mobile editor focus lifecycle, provision-form trigger restoration, and job-detail row restoration are implemented and regression-tested; the complete keyboard path was repeated through sign-in, roster, intake, processing, review, finalization, access, and jobs.

User Story: As a Screen-reader user, I want controls, progress, errors, selections, and status changes conveyed programmatically.
Expected Behavior: Pages have one useful H1, controls have accessible names, current navigation and selected credit are exposed, tables have headers, and asynchronous errors/progress/success use appropriate live announcements.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts; 2026-07-15 Chromium accessibility-tree inspection
Observed Errors: Automated names, states, headings, live regions, axe checks, and representative Chromium accessibility-tree inspection pass; an actual supported screen-reader session has not been recorded.

User Story: As a user on supported viewports, I want complete workflows without overlap or clipped actions.
Expected Behavior: At 360, 768, and 1280 CSS pixels and 200 percent zoom, text reflows, controls remain reachable, fixed-format elements remain stable, and no page has unintended horizontal scrolling.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts; tests/e2e/stress-layout.spec.ts

User Story: As a Product stakeholder, I want every approved screen and failure state rendered consistently with the design system.
Expected Behavior: All 45 canonical desktop and responsive states render the intended shell, typography, spacing, color, controls, content density, and state-specific actions without unrelated visual language.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts; tests/e2e/visual-smoke.spec.ts

User Story: As a user, I want interrupted reads and writes to fail honestly and recover without losing confirmed state.
Expected Behavior: Loading, offline, timeout, server, conflict, missing-video, and expired-session paths show safe actions; writes remain idempotent; retries never duplicate assessments, attempts, decisions, assignments, or final records.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/workflow-contracts.spec.ts; tests/e2e/screen-catalog.spec.ts; QA1 GCS/Eventarc/Vertex fail-closed smoke
Observed Errors: Synthetic failures plus live GCS/Eventarc/Vertex fail-closed behavior, managed-identity verification/reset/deactivation/recovery, immutable rollback, and secret-rotation recovery pass. Target-provider database point-in-time recovery, storage outage, and final scoring-contract/network-partition drills are not all recorded.
Fix Evidence: QA1 preserved one processing attempt with no partial result and redacted diagnostics. QA2 added provider recovery, shared abuse limits, 44-second rollback/forward, 14-second session-secret rotation, old-session rejection, and successful recovery sign-in.

User Story: As a Pilot user, I want core screens and interactions responsive on representative data and media.
Expected Behavior: Navigation and API latency stay within declared pilot budgets, list/search interactions avoid unnecessary network work, polling pauses in background tabs, video is range-streamed, and large rosters or suggestion sets remain usable.
Automated Evidence: tests/e2e/performance-contracts.spec.ts

User Story: As a Release manager, I want regressions blocked before deployment.
Expected Behavior: CI installs from the lockfile and requires format or lint, typecheck, unit, build, migration/schema, E2E behavior, accessibility, and selected visual checks with retained failure artifacts.
Automated Evidence: local full quality run; organization CI workflow execution
Observed Errors: The workflow gates 165 unit/service tests, catalogue validation, six migrations, shared-rate-limit concurrency, managed-identity production build, processor build, 46 behavioral, 6 accessibility, and 52 visual tests with failure artifacts. The complete post-QA2 local run passes; the organization-hosted CI run and artifact-retention observation are not yet recorded.
Fix Evidence: Added the managed configuration-loading regression, retained selected identity, catalogue, database, browser, accessibility, visual, Terraform, and diagnostics gates, and repeated the full suite after every QA2 identity/infrastructure/UX fix.

User Story: As a Pilot stakeholder, I want the exact deployed build proven across educator, administrator, privacy, failure, and recovery journeys before real data is enabled.
Expected Behavior: A versioned staging release passes every story in this tracker with approved identities and synthetic data, records defects and fixes, reruns the full suite after fixes, and has no open critical, high, logistical, or UX defect.
Automated Evidence: canonical tracker; full post-QA2 local regression; digest-pinned QA1 provider smoke; QA2 live managed identity and recovery drills
Observed Errors: Digest-pinned QA2 is deployed with managed identities and recovery evidence, but it is not yet the versioned permissioned staging release with authoritative content, Yi's accepted contract, approved roster, remaining provider drills, and required user/owner sign-offs.
Fix Evidence: The candidate has immutable web/processor digests, Ready revisions, 137 passing canonical stories, full local regression, managed provider setup/recovery/deactivation/reactivation, safe GCS/Eventarc/Vertex evidence, observability, rollback, secret rotation, and a clean Terraform plan.
